data.title: White House cryptocurrency advisor: Davos 2026 World Economic Forum is a turning point for the normalization of global cryptocurrencies.
data.description: White House cryptocurrency advisor Patrick Witt stated that the Davos 2026 World Economic Forum marked a turning point for the US government in transforming digital assets into a permanent asset class, with the president reiterating the commitment to building the US into a global crypto capital market. Witt pointed out that stablecoins have become an entry point into global finance, and the government is working to establish a symbiotic relationship between traditional finance and emerging crypto forces. Currently, the Senate Agriculture Committee is scheduled to review the Market Structures Act at 10:30 AM on January 29th. Although the Senate Banking Committee postponed its review due to issues such as stablecoin rewards, the legislation is expected to eventually reach a Senate vote. Furthermore, the government plans to shift its focus to developing a crypto tax scheme after the Market Structures Act is passed and will continue to monitor asset seizures related to national security operations in countries such as Venezuela.

data.title: US stocks open: Crypto sector performance is mixed, Strategy (MSTR) falls 1.07%
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.73%, the S&P 500 rose 0.19%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.42%. The crypto sector showed mixed performance: Solana rose 2.94%, SharpLink rose 1.06%, Bitmine rose 0.04%, Circle fell 1.36%, Coinbase fell 1.49%, and Strategy (MSTR) fell 1.07%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stocks and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Sees Mixed Opening as Corning and General Motors Shares Rise
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with mixed results, as the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell by 0.6%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite Index rose by 0.27% and 0.5%, respectively. According to ChainCatcher, Corning's stock surged by 7.8% following news that Meta will pay up to $6 billion in procurement fees. Additionally, General Motors saw a 5.4% increase in its stock price, driven by fourth-quarter core profits exceeding market expectations.

data.title: Grok service outage or access failure
data.description: X's AI assistant, Grok, is currently unusable, and users cannot access its functions through the platform interface.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Impact from U.S. Government Shutdown
data.description: Bitcoin traders are preparing for a possible U.S. government shutdown starting January 31, with prediction markets indicating up to an 80% chance of it occurring. According to NS3.AI, such a shutdown could delay the release of crucial economic data, heightening market uncertainty and potentially causing ETF outflows that may negatively affect Bitcoin prices. The extent of the shutdown's impact on Bitcoin could vary, with price fluctuations ranging from a minor 3% decrease to a significant 30% drop, potentially bringing prices down to $60,000.

data.title: AI Assistant Grok Faces Accessibility Issues
data.description: The AI assistant Grok on platform X is currently experiencing accessibility issues. According to Odaily, users are unable to access its features through the platform interface.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Leads $7 Million Investment in Tenbin for Tokenized Trading Markets
data.description: Galaxy Digital has spearheaded a $7 million investment round in Tenbin, focusing on the development of enhanced tokenized trading markets for gold and foreign exchange (FX) currencies. According to NS3.AI, Tenbin plans to utilize CME futures on blockchain infrastructure to facilitate quicker and more efficient trading of these tokenized assets. This initiative aims to connect traditional asset markets with blockchain technology, improving liquidity and reducing settlement times.

data.title: Miden and Korea Digital Asset Collaborate on Crypto Infrastructure
data.description: Miden, a blockchain project focused on privacy, has announced a partnership with Korea Digital Asset to advance crypto infrastructure for institutional adoption in South Korea. According to NS3.AI, the collaboration will prioritize privacy, regulatory compliance, and adherence to industry standards. The initiative seeks to promote the secure and regulated use of digital assets within institutional settings.

data.title: Bitfinex whales are accumulating positions at a rate of 400 to 600 BTC per day, and believe that adding to their positions at the current price is reasonable.
data.description: Benson Sun, founder of Coinkarma, posted on the X platform that Bitfinex whales have recently been accumulating positions at a rate of 400 to 600 BTC per day. OTC Desk recently injected approximately $300 million USDT into Bitfinex. This is Bitfinex's most aggressive accumulation since its price bottomed out on November 20th last year. This behavior typically indicates strong selling pressure in the market, resulting in minimal impact on price movements, and suggests that the market has entered a new phase. Currently, this entity believes that adding to positions at the current level is reasonable.

data.title: Bitget has listed the compliant Tether stablecoin USAT.
data.description: Bitget has listed Tether's compliant stablecoin USAT in its Innovation Zone. Trading is now open, and withdrawals will open on January 28th at 22:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: BRICS Nations Accelerate De-Dollarization Efforts
data.description: BRICS countries are actively reducing their US dollar reserves, which have decreased from 58.2% in 2024 to 56.92% in early 2026, signaling a significant shift towards de-dollarization. According to NS3.AI, central banks, led by BRICS, have purchased over 1,100 tons of gold in 2025, marking the largest increase in 70 years and challenging the dominance of the US dollar. As BRICS diversify into gold and local currencies, the US dollar's status as the world's primary reserve currency faces increasing threats.

data.title: Australian regulators have fined BPS Financial $9.7 million for irregularities related to Qoin wallet.
data.description: The Federal Court of Australia has ruled that BPS Financial is fined A$14 million (approximately US$9.7 million) for operating the Qoin wallet project without the required licenses and for making misleading statements to consumers.

data.title: ZetaChain 2.0 officially launched; Anuma creates a new AI × privacy platform.
data.description: ZetaChain announced that its privacy-focused AI interface, Anuma, has officially entered the testing phase and opened up a public candidate list. As its first consumer-facing application, Anuma supports seamless switching between multiple mainstream AI models while preserving privacy memories and allowing user control. Simultaneously, ZetaChain officially released ZetaChain 2.0, a new interoperability layer for AI designed to help developers build applications and agents that can operate across models and chains. Its core components include an AI portal and a Private Memory Layer, supporting privacy protection, access control, and global monetization capabilities.

data.title: Chainlink Labs Joins Global Alliance to Boost KRW Stablecoin Adoption
data.description: Chainlink Labs has become a member of the Global Alliance for KRW Stablecoin (GAKS), an initiative led by Wemade, to promote the adoption and infrastructure of Korean won-backed stablecoins. According to NS3.AI, the alliance aims to focus on technical standardization, compliance, and enhancing reliability in line with global financial standards. Chainlink's expertise in oracles and institutional-grade infrastructure is expected to support the development of next-generation stablecoins in Korea and the APAC region.

data.title: USD/JPY Experiences Short-Term Decline
data.description: The USD/JPY currency pair experienced a short-term decline, dropping over 30 points. According to ChainCatcher, the pair briefly fell below the 153 mark, with an intraday decrease of 0.75%.

data.title: Ethena: Proposed reducing the number of risk committee members from 5 to 3.
data.description: Ethena Labs Research has proposed a vote by ENA token holders before next week's election to reduce the size of the risk committee from five voting members to three. Ethena Labs Research believes that a smaller membership will allow for clearer division of labor, covering specific areas such as DeFi lending exposure, reserve funds and redemption requirements, protocol partner integration, and collateral assets. Furthermore, a smaller membership would allow the Ethena Foundation to increase member compensation, encouraging them to allocate more resources. If the proposal passes, ENA and sENA holders will elect three voting members, with Ethena Labs Research serving as an advisory member (non-voting member). If the proposal fails, the original plan will proceed with electing five members.

data.title: Ethena Labs Proposes Reduction in Risk Committee Members Ahead of Election
data.description:
Ethena Labs Research has proposed a vote by ENA token holders to reduce the number of voting members on the risk committee from five to three ahead of next week's election. According to Odaily, the research team believes that a smaller committee would allow for clearer division of responsibilities across specific areas such as DeFi lending exposure, reserve funds, redemption requirements, protocol partner integration, and collateral assets.

The proposal suggests that reducing the number of members would enable the Ethena Foundation to increase member compensation, encouraging them to dedicate more resources. If the proposal is approved, ENA and sENA holders will elect three voting members, while Ethena Labs Research will serve as an advisory non-voting member. If the proposal is not approved, the original plan to elect five members will proceed.

data.title: Animoca Brands Japan and RootstockLabs Collaborate on Bitcoin DeFi Tools for Japanese Market
data.description:
Animoca Brands Japan has announced a partnership with RootstockLabs to bring Bitcoin-native decentralized finance (DeFi) tools to Japanese corporations and institutions. According to Cointelegraph, the collaboration seeks to localize and implement Rootstock’s institutional program in Japan, allowing companies to incorporate Bitcoin (BTC) into their corporate treasury strategies while utilizing onchain financial tools secured by Bitcoin’s Proof-of-Work (PoW). This initiative was revealed in a Tuesday announcement.

Kensuke Amo, CEO of Animoca Brands Japan, highlighted the growing trend of Japanese companies integrating cryptoassets into their financial and treasury strategies. He stated that through this partnership, Animoca Brands Japan and RootstockLabs aim to facilitate corporate adoption of cryptoassets in compliance with Japan's regulatory environment. Rootstock, a Bitcoin sidechain, is secured by a significant portion of Bitcoin’s total hash power through merged mining, enabling it to inherit Bitcoin’s security while supporting Ethereum-compatible smart contracts. RootstockLabs plays a key role as a core contributor to Rootstock.

The partnership will concentrate on assisting Japanese companies in managing Bitcoin as part of their treasury operations. This includes exploring Bitcoin-based financial tools and services built on Rootstock. The companies plan to investigate how firms can use these tools to enhance treasury efficiency while adhering to Japan’s regulatory framework. They will evaluate the use of Rootstock-based assets such as Rootstock Bitcoin (rBTC), a Bitcoin-pegged token used in its DeFi ecosystem, and Rootstock Infrastructure Framework (RIF), a suite of utility protocols designed to scale Bitcoin by enabling faster, cheaper decentralized applications (dApps). Animoca Brands Japan may offer these services through its Digital Asset Treasury Management Support Service.

Japanese companies are increasingly viewing Bitcoin as a strategic reserve asset. Metaplanet stands out as a leading corporate adopter of a Bitcoin treasury strategy in Japan, holding 35,102 BTC. Other publicly traded Japanese companies with Bitcoin positions include NEXON Co., Ltd., with approximately 1,717 BTC, Remixpoint with about 1,411 BTC, and Anap Holdings Inc. with around 1,347 BTC. This growing interest underscores the potential for Bitcoin to become a significant component of corporate treasury strategies in Japan.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Companies Face Downgrades Amid AI Shift Concerns
data.description: Bitcoin mining companies HIVE, Bitfarm, and Bitdeer have been downgraded by analyst Stephen Glagola from KBW. According to NS3.AI, the downgrade stems from concerns that these companies' transition towards artificial intelligence (AI) and high-performance computing (HPC) may take longer to yield returns than anticipated by the market. This move highlights apprehensions regarding the pace and profitability of diversification strategies undertaken by crypto mining firms.

data.title: Chinese-Language Networks Linked to Significant Cryptocurrency Money Laundering
data.description: Chinese-language networks account for about 20% of cryptocurrency money laundering activities. According to NS3.AI, these networks are widespread, creating substantial obstacles for regulatory and law enforcement bodies. The report underscores the persistent dangers tied to illegal crypto activities within Chinese-speaking communities.
